prompt: What SPECIFICALLY makes cohere.com (Cohere, the AI company) read as a premium unicorn brand in 2025-2026? I need designer-grade detail, not vibes: the exact typefaces (and foundries) used for display vs body, the type scale and letter-spacing choices, the precise palette (hex values or close approximations designers cite), how imagery/illustration is art-directed (3D renders? editorial photography? abstract systems diagrams?), grid and whitespace decisions, nav and footer construction, and any signature motion. Quote designers/design reviews verbatim (@handle + date) on X and cite teardowns, Fonts In Use entries, awards pages, or agency case studies (which agency/studio designed it?). Every claim must be specific enough that a developer could replicate the move.
content:
**Pentagram (London) designed Cohere’s 2022–2023 rebrand and digital experience (still foundational to the 2025–2026 site), with custom type by NaN.** Partners Jody Hudson-Powell and Luke Powell led; NaN team included Luke Prowse, Jean-Baptiste Morizot, and Florian Runge.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)[[2]](https://www.nan.xyz/custom/cohere/)

The core concept is “New Nature”: fusing organic biology (cells, Voronoi tessellations from nature/math) with synthetic/computational elements to position enterprise language AI as practical and premium rather than experimental. This drives every visual decision for a sophisticated, tech-forward yet approachable unicorn aesthetic.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)

### Typeface (Custom Family by NaN + Pentagram)
- **Display/Headlines**: “Cohere Headline” (variable). Features Voronoi-inspired cell-like cuts/openings in characters that disperse automatically as you type. Variable axes control weight, number, and placement of cuts for precise, organic variation. A one-weight “Cohere Outline” variant is used specifically for certain headline applications (e.g., Cohere For AI).[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)
- **Body/Text**: “Cohere Text” in three weights (bold, regular, light) + italics. Includes a faux-mono style option referencing code/digital environments. Supports 306+ languages with built-in special characters, endpoint glyphs, and small tags/notes for contrast.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)
- **Mono/Code**: Dedicated “Cohere Mono” for code snippets and environments.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)
- **Implementation notes for replication**: Use the variable font for headlines with controlled cut parameters to echo Voronoi cells. Pair Text for body with tight tracking on display for premium polish. The wordmark itself is set in this family with cell openings as a core motif.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)

No public Fonts In Use entry for the AI company’s custom family (a separate construction firm uses F37 Zagma); details come directly from Pentagram’s case study and NaN’s project page.[[2]](https://www.nan.xyz/custom/cohere/)

### Color Palette
Natural tones meet synthetic accents for the “new nature” duality:
- Natural: Coniferous green, mushroom grey, volcanic black.
- Synthetic: Simulated coral, synthetic quartz, acrylic blue.
- Extension: Gradient “atmospheres” (layered, non-dominant textures) for layouts.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)

A design-system analysis notes a mostly white/near-black shell with cool rules, plus saturated color in dark product fields, coral editorial controls, and vibrant gradients for data-rich dashboards. Exact hex values are not publicly enumerated in case studies (descriptive names are used), but the dual natural/synthetic split and gradient use are signature.[[3]](https://getdesign.md/cohere/design-md)

### Imagery & Illustration Art Direction
- **Core system**: Voronoi diagrams/patterns (mathematical partitioning into cells, seen in nature like giraffe coats or dragonfly wings) as infinite tessellations. These appear across backgrounds, icons, and patterns.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)
- **3D elements**: Custom Cinema 4D tool (Python-based, no prior 3D knowledge needed) generates “pebble” visuals—organic, cell-like 3D forms with Voronoi influence, glass effects, texture offset, looping animations, and preset or custom palettes. Supports micro/macro views and wide/narrow framing.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)
- **Icons & pictograms**: Monolinear 24 px icons inspired by Voronoi; functional UI icons and endpoint pictograms (conversational “blocks” in cell language).
- **Overall**: Mix of 3D renders (pebbles), abstract systems diagrams (Voronoi/cells), and clean editorial/product photography or gradients. Avoids generic AI visuals in favor of biological-computational fusion.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)

Bespoke tools handed to Cohere’s in-house team: Figma plugin for Voronoi patterns, layout component libraries (cells in containers or forming around words), and After Effects integration for animation.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)

### Grid, Whitespace, Navigation & Footer
The site uses a clean, enterprise-grade structure with generous whitespace for breathing room and premium feel—typical of Pentagram digital work. Sections are modular and scannable (hero with strong headline, trust logos, product cards, industry use cases, developer resources, blog/news). Navigation is minimal/top-bar (standard enterprise: logo, links to products/solutions/resources, CTAs like “Go North” or “Learn more”). Footer is comprehensive but not overly dense, with links to docs, blog, security, etc. The overall grid supports data-rich dashboards and clear hierarchy without clutter.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)[[4]](https://cohere.com/)

### Signature Motion
- Animated Voronoi patterns and connecting cells.
- 3D pebble clusters (looping, viewable from multiple angles, with glass/texture effects).
- Cell/word animations via the After Effects tools and Figma/After Effects libraries.
- Subtle, purposeful motion that reinforces the organic-yet-computational “new nature” without being flashy.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)

This system (custom variable type with built-in brand motifs, dual palette, Voronoi/3D cell visuals, flexible tools) creates instant recognition and scalability—key to the premium unicorn positioning. The 2023 Pentagram work, including the website and playground, remains the backbone as of the current cohere.com.[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)[[1]](https://www.pentagram.com/work/cohere)

No verbatim X designer quotes surfaced in targeted searches for this specific site/brand teardown, but the Pentagram and NaN case studies provide the authoritative, replicable details above.
